page: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processors operate at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz and include 54 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. The hardware excels in parallelized workloads, as evidenced by top-tier results in ray tracing, image processing, and Passmark CPU extended instruction and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth for large-block read operations also ranks in the top 10%. Conversely, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% tier.

The c6i.metal instance offers high compute density and bare-metal access, eliminating virtualization overhead. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. The 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU ratio represents a tradeoff, prioritizing raw compute capacity over memory-intensive operations. This hardware profile is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound, highly parallelized workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. Suitable workloads include high-performance computing (HPC), batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs across 64 physical Intel Xeon 8375C cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.5 GHz, the instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, encryption capabilities, and large-block memory read bandwidth, though single-core performance remains average. The absence of local storage and a moderate memory-to-vCPU ratio represent key architectural tradeoffs. This instance is highly suited for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads such as batch processing, software compilation, static web serving, and high-performance computing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6i.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for high-performance parallel computing.
tagline: A bare-metal, compute-optimized platform featuring 128 vCPUs and high-bandwidth networking for demanding parallel processing workloads.

256 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per vCPU
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
